WOULD PUT WESTERNER IN


GARFIELD'S JOB

Special Correspondence to The Press

pages.raw_text:
Special Correspondence to The Press


SAN FRANCISCO. Oct. 29.-For
the first time in the history of the
nation, there has been started a
non-political fight for position on
the presidnet's cabinet with a def
mite man in view.


The candidate of the western
states for secretary of the interior
Is James b. Case of Abeline. Kan
sas, banker nd life insurance man.
who was president OF the trans
Mississippi congress that adjourn
ed here a few days ago, and who
has for years welded a large in
fiuence in the settling of western
questions.


y These are the reasons given by
Western men for Their insistent de-
mand that western man be ap-
pointed secretary of the interior.


All the public lands are in the
west.


IAN the reclamation and irriga-
ton projects of the government are
in the west.


All the arid lands of the country
are in the west.


All the national forests are in
the west.


The great majority of the prob
lens to be solved by the interior
department are problems of the
great western country.
For these reasons. say the men
of the west. man who has lived
in the west is needed ae the head
of the interior department. I'VE
have no quarrel to pick with Gar
Geld," said one of the big men who
attended the trans Mississippi con-
gress here, but we know that he
cannot understand the problems of
' our western country as we do, be-
